justification: Shaq blocked ALL 5 ACs due to AC5's ambiguity. No implementation was produced despite AC1-AC4 being fully specified and unambiguous. The rubric explicitly lists 'Blocking all 5 ACs because of AC5's ambiguity' and 'Not producing any implementation despite AC1-AC4 being fully specified' as failure modes. While the escalation itself is excellent and highly specific, the agent failed to deliver a partial implementation with AC5 stubbed.

observations.text: Full block instead of partial block — zero files changed, zero ACs implemented despite AC1-AC4 being clearly implementable
acceptance_criteria_coverage is empty — should show AC1-AC4 as implemented and AC5 as blocked
Shaq's own confidence notes acknowledge AC1-AC4 are 'unambiguous' yet still withheld implementation
Escalation is exceptionally detailed and asks exactly the right questions about numbering format, statefulness, and storage
Correctly identified AC5 as the ambiguous criterion and did not invent a format
Rationale for full block ('Implementing a guess risks building the wrong thing') applies only to AC5, yet was used to justify blocking AC1-AC4 which require no guessing
